research_series.series_name: Mission Phase and Temporal Dynamics Variation Tree

core_interpretation: Mission phase and within-person temporal dynamics are a global rule layer: individual baseline, repeated measures, temporal lag and recovery slope take priority over group reference ranges.
guardrails: Do not infer abnormal state from group reference range alone.
Do not infer pathology from early mission adaptation alone.
Do not interpret postflight values without recovery timing.
Do not ignore lag between exposure and symptom/marker response.
Do not compare phases without protocol/sample timing.
research_gaps: Need harmonized protocols and standardized reporting of timing/sampling across studies.
Limited longitudinal studies with sufficient sample size to model persistent deviations and recovery slopes.
Insufficient integration of multi-modal data across all mission phases.
Lack of robust methods to distinguish adaptation from pathology using time-series modeling.
